rationale: Ground truth is narrowly 'duplicate of #4808, see #4809 comment for details' — Y matches this precisely, correctly separating #4810 (AR-side, 4/5 sites) from #4808 (the specific DiT site this issue reports), and surfaces the actual nuance implied by 'additional details': #4808 shows closed/unmerged and the #4810 regression test has a coverage hole for this exact file. X instead confidently declares both PRs 'merged' and the bug fully fixed, but its own diagnostics show it never ran the suggested test and never verified GitHub PR state — it conflates #4808/#4810 and asserts specifics (line numbers, test names) not backed by verification, which is risky given Y's finding that #4808 may not have merged at all. Y's hedged, well-cited escalation is closer to how a maintainer would actually respond to a duplicate report.
